The applicants have submitted a development proposal for 26-34 Nipigon Avenue (the 'subject lands'), generally located east of Yonge Street, south of Steeles Avenue East. The subject lands are presently developed with 5 single detached dwellings, and are located directly south of an existing TTC bus loop. The applicants have submitted a proposal for a 50-storey residential building, containing 569 units, 38,099.89 square metres of residential gross floor area, and having a density of 17.0 times the area of lot. The proposal contains 3 levels of underground parking, 132 vehicular parking spaces and 483 bicycle parking spaces. The proposal contains 1,724.88 square metres of indoor amenity space, and 671.05 square metres of outdoor amenity space.

OLT settlement which contemplates a mixed residential/retail development of five buildings, ranging in height from 17-storeys (with a six-storey mid-rise component) to 49-storeys, with approximately 2,100 dwelling units including a unit mix of 15% two-bedroom units and 10% three-bedroom units. A total of approximately 130,260 sq m of Gross Floor Area (GFA) is proposed, inclusive of 985 sq m of non-residential GFA, resulting in a gross density of 4.95 Floor Space index (FSI). An Official Plan Amendment and Zoning By-law Amendment application has been made for 7 - 17 Nipigon Avenue (the "subject lands"), generally located east of Yonge Street, south of Steeles Avenue East, on the south side of Nipigon Avenue. The subject lands are presently developed with 5 single detached dwellings.The proposal is seeking to permit a 52-storey tall building with a 5-storey base building on the subject lands. The proposal contains 603 dwelling units and 263 square metres of non-residential gross floor area. On September 2, 2025, the Ontario Land Tribunal ("OLT") rendered an Interim Decision approving the proposal. The applicant's are now working through the conditions of approval.

Site Plan Application in relation to rezoning application file #16 198950 WET 06 OZ. This application proposes four apartment buildings (22, 30, 36, and 36 storeys) connected by a five-storey mixed-use base building to be constructed in two phases. Phase 1 will include integrated Metrolinx infrastructure for the GO Transit Station including a new station building, pedestrian pick-up drop-off (PPUDO) facility, transit plaza, and underground commuter parking. The proposed buildings (both phases combined) would have 1,351 residential units, 2,240.7 square metres of non-residential gross floor area, 614 car parking spaces underground (not including the 300 proposed Metrolinx parking spaces), and 1,056 bicycle parking spaces (not including the 66 proposed Metrolinx bicycle parking spaces).
